title: betula davurica var. typica
intro: Betula davurica var. typica, also known as the Korean birch or Manchurian birch, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the birch family (Betulaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits of Betula davurica var. typica include its potential to reduce fever, relieve pain and inflammation, and treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of Betula davurica var. typica include tea infusions, tinctures, and poultices, which are used to apply its medicinal properties topically or internally.
